Nerve Injury & Brachial Plexus

Hand Surgery Education · Week 2 Study Guide
⏱ 0:00 🔥 Streak 0

Welcome — let's calibrate.

This guide covers adult nerve injury and brachial plexus reconstruction, built entirely from your Week 2 reading list (Noland JAAOS 2019, Moore PRS 2014, Huayllani Ann Plast Surg 2020, Weber/Isaacs JAAOS 2023, Barbour/Mackinnon JHS 2012, Isaacs/Cochran BJJ 2019, plus the Green's and Handthology chapters).

How comfortable are you with this material?

🌱 Beginner

Start with a structured study guide (diagrams + key concepts), then move into questions when you're ready.

⚡ Expert

Skip ahead. Go straight into multiple-choice questions and flashcards to test mastery.

Session ends automatically at 30 minutes, after 10 correct in a row, or whenever you hit Done reviewing.

Study Guide

Review at your own pace. When you're ready, jump into questions.

❓ Quiz me

Sources (Week 2 reading list):

Some diagrams are original schematics created for this guide; others (figures and clinical/intraoperative photos, credited under each image) are reproduced directly from the Week 2 source texts — Green's Operative Hand Surgery 6th ed. (select figures by permission of Mayo Foundation for Medical Education and Research, all rights reserved), the Handthology chapters, and the cited journal articles — for internal educational use only, not for external distribution or publication. All facts and figures are drawn from the sources above; this tool is for educational review and is not a substitute for primary literature or clinical judgment.
